title: elymus viridiglumis
intro: Elymus viridiglumis, also known as slough grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of essential oils. The medicinal properties of Elymus viridiglumis have been found to aid in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, Elymus viridiglumis can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves that can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
